name: σFlow-PDE: H-Bar Phase Engine for Compositional Neural Operators
description: A drop-in H-Bar training engine that escapes the σ-trap in neural PDE solvers via live σ/δ/α ODE integration, autonomous phase curriculum, and auto-falsification.

hypotheses.statement: Live σ/δ/α ODE integration during training escapes low-frequency spectral traps (σ-trap), enabling convergence to higher-frequency solution components compared to standard training.
hypotheses.falsification_criterion: Mean relative L2 error on high-frequency holdout PDEs ≤ 5% worse than best-in-class baseline (FNO/DeepONet) after convergence.

hypotheses.statement: Autonomous phase curriculum adaptively schedules training phases based on spectral convergence diagnostics, reducing total training steps to convergence by ≥ 30% relative to fixed-schedule training.
hypotheses.falsification_criterion: Training step reduction < 30% or final accuracy degradation > 2% relative to fixed-schedule baseline.

hypotheses.statement: Auto-falsification detects and rejects models that fail spectral consistency checks, ensuring 100% Falsifiability Quotient across 24-split PDE grammar benchmarks.
hypotheses.falsification_criterion: Any trained model passes spectral consistency checks yet exhibits OOD relative L2 error > 15% on the holdout test set.

methodology.framework: H-Bar training dynamics with coupled σ (spectral density), δ (phase offset), α (learning rate adaptation) ODEs
methodology.benchmark: 24-split PDE grammar (advection, diffusion, Burger's, wave, elliptic, mixed PDE families)
